# Proverbs 16

## Verses (33)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | The designs of the heart are man's, but the answer of the tongue comes from the Lord. | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | All a man's ways are clean to himself; but the Lord puts men's spirits into his scales. | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | Put your works into the hands of the Lord, and your purposes will be made certain. | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | The Lord has made everything for his purpose, even the sinner for the day of evil. | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | Everyone who has pride in his heart is disgusting to the Lord: he will certainly not go free from punishment. | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | By mercy and good faith evil-doing is taken away: and by the fear of the Lord men are turned away from evil. | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | When a man's ways are pleasing to the Lord, he makes even his haters be at peace with him. | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | Better is a little with righteousness, than great wealth with wrongdoing. | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | A man may make designs for his way, but the Lord is the guide of his steps. | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | Decision is in the lips of the king: his mouth will not go wrong in judging. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | True measures and scales are the Lord's: all the weights of the bag are his work. | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | Evil-doing is disgusting to kings: for the seat of the ruler is based on righteousness. | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | Lips of righteousness are the delight of kings; and he who says what is upright is dear to him. | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | The wrath of the king is like those who give news of death, but a wise man will put peace in place of it. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | In the light of the king's face there is life; and his approval is like a cloud of spring rain. | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | How much better it is to get wisdom than gold! and to get knowledge is more to be desired than silver. | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | The highway of the upright is to be turned away from evil: he who takes care of his way will keep his soul. | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | Pride goes before destruction, and a stiff spirit before a fall. | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | Better it is to have a gentle spirit with the poor, than to take part in the rewards of war with men of pride. | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | He who gives attention to the law of right will get good; and whoever puts his faith in the Lord is happy. | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
| [21](BBE.md#v21) | The wise-hearted will be named men of good sense: and by pleasing words learning is increased. | [WEB](WEB.md#v21) · [KJV](KJV.md#v21) · [ASV](ASV.md#v21) |
| [22](BBE.md#v22) | Wisdom is a fountain of life to him who has it; but the punishment of the foolish is their foolish behaviour. | [WEB](WEB.md#v22) · [KJV](KJV.md#v22) · [ASV](ASV.md#v22) |
| [23](BBE.md#v23) | The heart of the wise man is the teacher of his mouth, and gives increased learning to his lips. | [WEB](WEB.md#v23) · [KJV](KJV.md#v23) · [ASV](ASV.md#v23) |
| [24](BBE.md#v24) | Pleasing words are like honey, sweet to the soul and new life to the bones. | [WEB](WEB.md#v24) · [KJV](KJV.md#v24) · [ASV](ASV.md#v24) |
| [25](BBE.md#v25) | There is a way which seems straight before a man, but its end is the ways of death. | [WEB](WEB.md#v25) · [KJV](KJV.md#v25) · [ASV](ASV.md#v25) |
| [26](BBE.md#v26) | The desire of the working man is working for him, for his need of food is driving him on. | [WEB](WEB.md#v26) · [KJV](KJV.md#v26) · [ASV](ASV.md#v26) |
| [27](BBE.md#v27) | A good-for-nothing man is a designer of evil, and in his lips there is a burning fire. | [WEB](WEB.md#v27) · [KJV](KJV.md#v27) · [ASV](ASV.md#v27) |
| [28](BBE.md#v28) | A man of twisted purposes is a cause of fighting everywhere: and he who says evil secretly makes trouble between friends. | [WEB](WEB.md#v28) · [KJV](KJV.md#v28) · [ASV](ASV.md#v28) |
| [29](BBE.md#v29) | A violent man puts desire of evil into his neighbour's mind, and makes him go in a way which is not good. | [WEB](WEB.md#v29) · [KJV](KJV.md#v29) · [ASV](ASV.md#v29) |
| [30](BBE.md#v30) | He whose eyes are shut is a man of twisted purposes, and he who keeps his lips shut tight makes evil come about. | [WEB](WEB.md#v30) · [KJV](KJV.md#v30) · [ASV](ASV.md#v30) |
| [31](BBE.md#v31) | The grey head is a crown of glory, if it is seen in the way of righteousness. | [WEB](WEB.md#v31) · [KJV](KJV.md#v31) · [ASV](ASV.md#v31) |
| [32](BBE.md#v32) | He who is slow to be angry is better than a man of war, and he who has control over his spirit than he who takes a town. | [WEB](WEB.md#v32) · [KJV](KJV.md#v32) · [ASV](ASV.md#v32) |
| [33](BBE.md#v33) | A thing may be put to the decision of chance, but it comes about through the Lord. | [WEB](WEB.md#v33) · [KJV](KJV.md#v33) · [ASV](ASV.md#v33) |
